question: What does the Simple Present express?
options: Habits, routines, general truths (I go to school every day. Water boils at 100 degrees.)
explanation: Simple Present: 1) Gewohnheiten: "I play football on Sundays." 2) Allgemeine Wahrheiten: "The sun rises in the east." 3) Fahrplaene/Programme: "The train leaves at 8."

question: How do you form the Simple Present for he/she/it?
options: Add -s or -es (He plays, She watches, It goes)
explanation: 3rd person singular (he/she/it): add -s. Most verbs: play -> plays. Verbs ending in s/x/ch/sh/o: add -es. watch -> watches. go -> goes. Irregular: have -> has.

question: How do you form Simple Present questions?
options: Do/Does + subject + infinitive? (Do you like pizza? Does she play chess?)
explanation: Simple Present questions: Do (I/you/we/they) + infinitive? Does (he/she/it) + infinitive? "Do you know her?" "Does he live here?" Do NOT add -s after does.

question: What is the Simple Past used for?
options: Completed actions in the past at a specific time (Yesterday, I went to the cinema)
explanation: Simple Past: abgeschlossene Handlungen in der Vergangenheit, oft mit Zeitangabe: yesterday, last week, in 1999, ago. "I visited Berlin last summer."

question: What does "just" indicate in the Present Perfect?
options: Something happened very recently (She has just arrived)
explanation: "Just" + Present Perfect: etwas ist gerade eben passiert. "He has just called you." "I have just finished my homework." Position: between have/has and the past participle.

question: What does "already" indicate in the Present Perfect?
options: Something happened sooner than expected (I have already eaten)
explanation: "Already" = schon/bereits. "I have already done my homework." Position: usually between have/has and past participle, or at end. Used for affirmatives.

question: What does "yet" indicate in the Present Perfect?
options: Something expected has NOT happened (I haven't finished yet) OR asking if expected thing happened (Have you eaten yet?)
explanation: "Yet" = noch nicht (negativ) / schon (Frage). "I haven't done my homework yet." "Have you called her yet?" Position: end of sentence.

question: What does "ever" and "never" indicate with Present Perfect?
options: Ever: in your life experience (Have you ever been to Spain?). Never: not in your life.
explanation: "Ever" = jemals (in Fragen): "Have you ever eaten sushi?" "Never" = nie: "I have never seen snow." Used for life experiences.

question: What is the Past Progressive (Verlaufsform der Vergangenheit)?
options: An ongoing action at a specific time in the past (I was watching TV when the phone rang)
explanation: Past Progressive: was/were + -ing. Beschreibt laufende Handlung zu einem bestimmten Zeitpunkt in der Vergangenheit. "At 8pm, she was cooking dinner." Often with Simple Past for interruption.

question: How do you form the Past Progressive?
explanation: Past Progressive: was (I/he/she/it) + verb-ing OR were (you/we/they) + verb-ing. "I was sleeping." "They were playing football."

question: How is the Past Progressive used with the Simple Past?
options: Past Progressive = background/ongoing action; Simple Past = interruption (I was sleeping when the doorbell rang)
Past Progressive is always after Simple Past
explanation: Kombination: "While I was walking home, it started to rain." Past Progressive = laufende Hintergrundhandlung. Simple Past = kurze Unterbrechung.

question: What is the Past Perfect?
options: had + past participle - used for actions completed BEFORE another past action (I had eaten before she arrived)
explanation: Past Perfect: had + Partizip II. Vorvergangenheit. "When I arrived, she had already left." Ereignis 1 (she left) geschah BEVOR Ereignis 2 (I arrived). Signalwoerter: after, before, when, by the time.

question: Which sentence uses the Past Perfect correctly?
options: By the time she arrived, we had already eaten.
explanation: "By the time she arrived, we had already eaten." Correct: had + past participle (eaten). "By the time" = bis zu dem Zeitpunkt, als... -> Past Perfect usual.

question: What does the Future with "will" express?
options: Spontaneous decisions, predictions, offers, promises (I will help you! It will rain tomorrow.)
explanation: "Will" Future: 1) Spontane Entscheidungen: "I will answer that!" 2) Vorhersagen: "It will be sunny." 3) Versprechen: "I will always love you." 4) Angebote: "I will carry that for you."

question: When do we use "going to" instead of "will"?
options: Going to: for plans already made or evidence-based predictions (Look at those clouds - it's going to rain!)
Going to is always more polite
Will is for plans, going to is for spontaneous decisions
explanation: "Going to": Plan schon festgelegt: "I am going to visit my aunt on Sunday." Voraussage mit Beweis: "Look! He's going to fall!" Unterschied zu will: Spontan vs. geplant.

question: Which time expressions are typically used with the Simple Past?
options: yesterday, last week, in 2010, three days ago, when I was young
explanation: Simple Past Signalwoerter: yesterday, last (week/month/year), ago, in [year], when I was (a child), once, at that time.

question: Which time expressions are typically used with the Present Perfect?
options: already, just, yet, ever, never, recently, so far, this year
explanation: Present Perfect Signalwoerter: already, just, yet, ever, never, recently, lately, so far, this (week/month/year), since, for.

question: What is "since" used with?
options: A point in time with Present Perfect (I have lived here since 2015)
A specific date with Simple Past
explanation: "Since" + Zeitpunkt: "since 2010, since Monday, since she left." Used with Present Perfect. "I have known her since we were children." Compare: for + duration.

question: What is "for" used for in time expressions?
options: A duration of time (for three years, for a long time, for two weeks)
explanation: "For" + Zeitdauer: "for two hours, for a week, for ten years." With Present Perfect: "I have lived here for five years." Also Simple Past: "She worked there for a year."

question: What tense do we use to tell a story (narrative tense)?
options: Simple Past (and sometimes Past Progressive for background)
explanation: Ertzaehlerische Vergangenheit: Simple Past. "Once upon a time, there was a princess. She lived in a castle. One day, a knight arrived..." Past Progressive fuer Hintergrundbeschreibungen.

question: What is the future progressive?
options: Will + be + -ing - used for ongoing future actions (I will be studying all evening)
explanation: Future Progressive: will be + -ing. Zukunftige Handlung in Verlauf zu einem bestimmten Zeitpunkt. "This time tomorrow, I will be flying to London." Betont den Verlauf.

question: How do we express a scheduled/timetabled future event?
options: Simple Present (The train leaves at 8. The film starts at 7.)
explanation: Fahrplaene/Programme/Spielplaene: Simple Present. "The bus leaves at 9:15." "School starts on Monday." "The film finishes at 10pm." Feste Zeiten im Kalender.

question: What is the main difference between Simple Past and Present Perfect?
options: Simple Past: finished, specific time in past (yesterday). Present Perfect: connected to now, no specific time / life experience.
explanation: Simple Past: abgeschlossen, spez. Zeitpunkt. "I saw her yesterday." Present Perfect: Verbindung zur Gegenwart, kein spez. Zeitpunkt. "I have seen her." (Leben, Ergebnis jetzt wichtig).

question: What does "How long have you...?" ask about?
options: Duration of an action starting in the past and still continuing (How long have you lived here? - For 5 years.)
explanation: "How long have you + Past Participle?" fragt nach Dauer einer Handlung, die begann und noch laeuft. "How long have you known her?" "I have known her for 3 years." Antwort: for/since.

question: What tense do we usually use after time conjunctions like "when, before, after, until" for future?
options: Simple Present (I will call you when I arrive - NOT when I will arrive)
explanation: Nach Zeitkonjunktionen (when, before, after, until, as soon as) fuer Zukunft: Simple Present! "When she comes, we will eat." NOT "When she will come".

question: What is the Past Perfect used for with "after" and "before"?
options: It is never used with these words
To show the sequence of two past events (After she had finished her work, she left)
explanation: Past Perfect mit after/before: zeigt Reihenfolge. "After he had eaten, he went to bed." (essen kam zuerst). "Before I arrived, she had already left." Hatte schon getan.

question: Which verb form follows a modal verb?
options: The infinitive without "to" (can swim, must go, should eat)
explanation: Modal verbs + bare infinitive (without to): can swim, must go, should eat. NOT: can swimming, must goes, should to go.

question: How do you say "koennte" (past ability/polite) in English?
options: could (I could swim when I was five / Could you help me?)
explanation: "Could" = koennte/konnte. 1) Past ability: "When I was young, I could run fast." 2) Polite request: "Could you help me?" 3) Possibility: "It could rain."

question: What is the difference between "must" and "have to" in questions/negatives?
options: Must not = forbidden. Don't have to = not necessary (You must not smoke here / You don't have to come)
Have to is never used in questions
explanation: "Must not/Mustn't": verboten. "You mustn't run here." "Don't have to/Doesn't have to": nicht noetig. "You don't have to wear a tie." Completely different meaning!

question: What does "might" express?
options: Possibility - something may or may not happen (It might rain later - I'm not sure)
explanation: "Might" = koennte, vielleicht. Schwache Moeglichkeit. "I might go to the party." Less certain than "may". "It might snow tomorrow."

question: Which sentence is in the correct future form for a promise?
options: I am going to love you forever.
I will always be there for you.
explanation: "Will" fuer Versprechen und Schwuere: "I will always be there for you." "I will never forget you." Spontane Entscheidung/Versprechen -> will.

question: What does "I was about to..." mean?
options: I was just going to do something when something else happened (I was about to leave when the phone rang)
explanation: "Was/were about to" = wollte gerade... "I was about to call you when you called me." Drueckt aus, dass eine Handlung kurz vor der Ausfuehrung war, als etwas anderes geschah.

question: How do you form the Present Perfect with "since" to show duration?
options: Have/has + past participle + since + time point (I have lived here since 2010)
Have + been + -ing (also possible for ongoing action)
explanation: Present Perfect + since: "She has worked here since 2018." Aktion begann 2018 und dauert bis jetzt an. Auch: Present Perfect Progressive (emphasizes duration): "She has been working here since 2018."

question: What does "used to + infinitive" vs "would + infinitive" describe?
options: Both describe past habits, but "used to" can also describe past states, while "would" is for past actions only
Used to is for the future
explanation: "Used to": vergangene Zustaende UND Gewohnheiten. "I used to live in Berlin." (Zustand) "Would": nur vergangene Gewohnheiten. "Every Sunday, he would cook a big meal." NOT "I would live in Berlin."

question: What is the "narrative present"?
options: Using present tenses to tell past events more vividly (And then he opens the door and sees her!)
The usual way to tell stories
explanation: Historisches Praesens: Vergangenes im Praesens erzaehlen um Lebhaftigkeit zu erzeugen. "So I go to the shop, and suddenly I see him!" Gesprochene Sprache, lebhafte Erzaehlung.

question: What does the "present simple" express in headlines?
options: Past events described as present for dramatic effect (Earthquake hits Japan - happened in past)
explanation: Zeitungsschlagzeilen: Simple Present fuer abgeschlossene Ereignisse. "Man Finds Treasure in Garden." "Fire Destroys Hotel." Praesens macht Schlagzeilen kurz und dynamisch.

question: What is the Future Perfect?
options: Will + have + past participle - for actions completed before a future time (By 2025, I will have finished school)
explanation: Future Perfect: will + have + Partizip II. "By next year, I will have lived here for 10 years." Handlung wird vor einem bestimmten Zeitpunkt in der Zukunft abgeschlossen sein.

question: What does "hardly/scarcely/barely...when/before" express?
options: As soon as something happened, something else immediately followed (Hardly had she sat down when the phone rang)
explanation: "Hardly/scarcely/barely...when": kaum...als. Inversion noetig: "Hardly had I arrived when it started raining." = "I had hardly arrived when it started raining."

question: Which tense is correct in: "By the time you read this, I ___ already ___."?
explanation: "By the time you read this, I will have already left." Future Perfect fuer Handlung, die BEVOR einem Zeitpunkt in der Zukunft abgeschlossen sein wird.

question: What is "to be + past participle" in terms of tense/voice?
options: Passive voice (The book is read by many people)
explanation: be + past participle = Passiv. Simple Present Passiv: "English is spoken worldwide." Simple Past Passiv: "The letter was written." Present Perfect Passiv: "The bridge has been built."
